title: Decide production delivery and basemap provider boundary
goal: Make an explicit evidence-based decision on whether the current static Pages architecture and public OpenFreeMap instance are authorized for production responsibility, including failure, privacy, cost, attribution and fallback consequences.

approval.note: Use an isolated Commonworld worktree. Before merge require a complete downloadable diff, head- and diff-bound self-review, green CI and exact post-merge live validation.

acceptance.assertion: The current GitHub Pages, DNS, CSP, runtime origins, OpenFreeMap usage, attribution, caching and operational ownership are re-read live and documented before deciding.
Provider and self-hosting options are compared on SLA or explicit lack of SLA, privacy, terms, attribution, rate limits, cost, observability, fallback, data freshness and operational burden.
A named authority explicitly authorizes one bounded production architecture/provider contract or records a deferral and the conditions required to decide later.
The chosen or deferred path states behavior for provider outage, rate limiting, stale tiles, DNS/Pages failure and rollback without inventing availability guarantees.
This task produces a decision and validation contract only; provider migration, backend introduction or data ingestion requires a separately reviewed implementation task.
